items.verse: أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى ٱلْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ
items.verseEn: Then do they not look at the camels - how they are created?

items.verse: أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ
items.verseEn: Unquestionably, it is they who are the corrupters, but they perceive [it] not.

items.verse: وَمَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَآءًۭ وَنِدَآءًۭ ۚ صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌۭ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
items.verseEn: The example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts at what hears nothing but calls and cries [i.e., cattle or sheep] - deaf, dumb and blind, so they do not understand.

items.verse: لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًۭا وَلَا شَرَابًا
items.verseEn: They will not taste therein [any] coolness or drink.

categories.name: نَبَاتَاتٌ مُحَدَّدَةٌ ذُكِرَتْ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Specific plants mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: فَأَعْرَضُوا۟ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ ٱلْعَرِمِ وَبَدَّلْنَـٰهُم بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَوَاتَىْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍۢ وَأَثْلٍۢ وَشَىْءٍۢ مِّن سِدْرٍۢ قَلِيلٍۢ
items.verseEn: But they turned away [refusing], so We sent upon them the flood of the dam, and We replaced their two [fields of] gardens with gardens of bitter fruit, tamarisks and something of sparse lote trees.
At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary -
And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.

items.verse: يَـٰبُنَىَّ إِنَّهَآ إِن تَكُ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍۢ مِّنْ خَرْدَلٍۢ فَتَكُن فِى صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ أَوْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ يَأْتِ بِهَا ٱللَّهُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: [And Luqmān said], "O my son, indeed if it [i.e., a wrong] should be the weight of a mustard seed and should be within a rock or [anywhere] in the heavens or in the earth, Allāh will bring it forth. Indeed, Allāh is Subtle and Aware.

items.verse: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ رَفْرَفٍ خُضْرٍۢ وَعَبْقَرِىٍّ حِسَانٍۢ
items.verseEn: Reclining on green cushions and beautiful fine carpets.

items.verse: هُمْ وَأَزْوَٰجُهُمْ فِى ظِلَـٰلٍ عَلَى ٱلْأَرَآئِكِ مُتَّكِـُٔونَ
items.verseEn: They and their spouses - in shade, reclining on adorned couches.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ تَدُلُّ عَلَى حَالَةٍ قُصْوَى
categories.nameEn: Words denoting a state of extremity

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تُبْطِلُوا۟ صَدَقَـٰتِكُم بِٱلْمَنِّ وَٱلْأَذَىٰ كَٱلَّذِى يُنفِقُ مَالَهُۥ رِئَآءَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَا يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۖ فَمَثَلُهُۥ كَمَثَلِ صَفْوَانٍ عَلَيْهِ تُرَابٌۭ فَأَصَابَهُۥ وَابِلٌۭ فَتَرَكَهُۥ صَلْدًۭا ۖ لَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍۢ مِّمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, do not invalidate your charities with reminders [of it] or injury as does one who spends his wealth [only] to be seen by the people and does not believe in Allāh and the Last Day. His example is like that of a [large] smooth stone upon which is dust and is hit by a downpour that leaves it bare. They are unable [to keep] anything of what they have earned. And Allāh does not guide the disbelieving people.

items.verse: وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَن ذِكْرِى فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةًۭ ضَنكًۭا وَنَحْشُرُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ أَعْمَىٰ
items.verseEn: And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed [i.e., difficult] life, and We will gather [i.e., raise] him on the Day of Resurrection blind."

items.verse: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا وَفَارَ ٱلتَّنُّورُ قُلْنَا ٱحْمِلْ فِيهَا مِن كُلٍّۢ زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَن سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَوْلُ وَمَنْ ءَامَنَ ۚ وَمَآ ءَامَنَ مَعَهُۥٓ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌۭ
items.verseEn: [So it was], until when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, "Load upon it [i.e., the ship] of each [creature] two mates and your family, except those about whom the word [i.e., decree] has preceded, and [include] whoever has believed." But none had believed with him, except a few.

harf.hint: A place of refuge for the youth.
harf.arabicVerse: أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا عَجَبًا
harf.verse: 18:9 — Or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder?

deduction.title: The Reluctant Messenger
deduction.intro: Destiny calls upon a soul living in self-imposed exile. Fleeing a past transgression, this individual is chosen for a monumental task: to confront the most powerful tyrant of the age. With divine signs and a trusted companion, this messenger must return to the land of their youth to deliver a message of liberation and monotheism. Can you uncover the identity of this reluctant hero?
clues: I was raised in the heart of my enemy's household, yet I never knew them as my own.
In a moment of anger, I struck a person and unintentionally caused their death, forcing me to flee my home.
While traveling with my family, I saw a fire on a distant mountain and approached it, seeking warmth and guidance.
At that mountain, I spoke directly with my Lord, who commanded me to go to a tyrant and ask him to free my people.
My Lord granted me two great signs: my staff, which could turn into a serpent, and my hand, which would shine with a brilliant white light.
I asked my Lord to appoint my brother, Harun, as a helper for me, as he was more eloquent in speech.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Yusuf
Prophet Ibrahim
Prophet Musa
Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)
Dhul-Qarnayn

trial.label: What was my primary mission?
options: Building a great ark to survive a flood
Interpreting the dreams of a king in Egypt
Leading an army against the giant Jalut
Confronting Fir'awn and freeing the Israelites
Rebuilding the Kaaba with my son
trial.answer: Confronting Fir'awn and freeing the Israelites

location.label: What object was a key sign for me?
options: A coat of many colors that caused jealousy
A staff that turned into a serpent
A ship built on dry land
A ring that controlled the wind and Jinn
A clay bird that came to life
location.answer: A staff that turned into a serpent

outcome.label: What was a major event following the confrontation?
options: Parting the Red Sea to escape
Being thrown into a fire which became cool and safe
Being swallowed by a giant fish and repenting
Being raised to the heavens without dying
Being given a kingdom that no one after me would have
outcome.answer: Parting the Red Sea to escape

deduction.arabic: ٱسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِى جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍۢ وَٱضْمُمْ إِلَيْكَ جَنَاحَكَ مِنَ ٱلرَّهْبِ ۖ فَذَٰنِكَ بُرْهَـٰنَانِ مِن رَّبِّكَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۭا فَـٰسِقِينَ
deduction.verse: Insert your hand into the opening of your garment; it will come out white, without disease. And draw in your arm close to you [as prevention] from fear, for those are two proofs from your Lord to Pharaoh and his establishment. Indeed, they have been a people defiantly disobedient." (28:32)

translations: And (do) not turn your cheek from men
and (do) not walk in the earth
scramble.hint: A piece of advice from Luqman to his son about the importance of humility and avoiding arrogance.
scramble.arabic: وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍۢ فَخُورٍۢ
scramble.verseEn: And do not turn your cheek [in contempt] toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly. Indeed, Allāh does not like everyone self-deluded and boastful.
